Summary: Functions that use gpuArray fail the first time that they are executed. For example: rand(1,'gpuArray')
Description: The gpuArray  class is not properly loaded the first time that it is accessed. This problem causes an error in functions that require the class. Subsequent attempts to access the class are successful.
Workaround: If you get an error when executing functions that use gpuArray, execute the command a second time.

Summary: Data range specifications (DRS) for autosar.tlc targets contain the wrong function names
Description: When analyzing a model, Polyspace creates a data range specification file to use your design min/max values in the verification. If your model is configured for an autosar.tlc target, the generated data range specification file contains the wrong function names. Because the names do not match the generated code, user-defined design min/max values do not get applied during the code verification.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your Polyspace installation. By default it is C:\Program Files\MATLAB on Windows or /usr/local/MATLAB on Linux systems. This folder is referred as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Close the Polyspace verification environment (Polyspace UI).Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store it in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file:Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file.Windows Systems: Open the .zip file. Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:cd <POLYSPACE>unzip <.zip file>Check that binaries have appropriate 'read & execute' permissions.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Reopen the Polyspace verification environment.?

Summary: RESAMPLE now accounts for processing gain of the internal resampling filter.
Description: RESAMPLE now accounts for the DC processing gain of the internal resampling filter used. A simple way to see the change is to apply resampling to a constant value signal and view the results: >> % pre R2015a... >> y = resample(ones(1,1000),1,14,20,1);>> mean(y(25:45))? ans =? 0.9920? >> % R2015a and later...>> y = resample(ones(1,1000),1,14,20,1); >> mean(y(25:45))? ans = 1

Summary: Error "Unexpected MJS security level -2147483648" when launching Polyspace
Description: If you launch a remote verification and the scheduler is not the Matlab Job Scheduler, you can have the error:  Error: Unexpected MJS security level -2147483648
Workaround: Add the option -no-credentials-check to the list of analysis options that you use. If you are running analysis from the user interface, in your Polyspace project configuration, select Advanced Settings. Enter this option in the field Other.?

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Horizontal Wind Model 07 block code generation and latitude/longitude wrapping issues
Description: The Horizontal Wind Model 07 block has the following issues: When the model executes in Normal mode, longitude inputs below -180 degrees may cause MATLAB to stop responding. When the model executes in Normal mode, latitude inputs below -180 degrees cause the output values to be incorrect. When the block Units  parameter is set to an English  option, execution of the model in a code generation mode (Accelerator, Rapid Accelerator, External, and so forth) does not generate code that reflects this change. The outputs are the same as if the Metric (MKS)  option was selected. The model does not generate errors to indicate the issue. When the model executes in any of the code generation modes (Accelerator, Rapid Accelerator, External, and so forth) the output values differ from the output for the Normal mode (including NaN s). When the block is connected directly to undefined Inport ports or ground ports, execution of the model in a code generation mode (Accelerator, Rapid Accelerator, External, and so forth) returns an error. When getting the block object parameter Capabilities , MATLAB stops responding.

Summary: Misleading error message is thrown when referring to an undefined enumerator
Description: If you use an undefined enumerator in a Stateflow chart using C as the Action Language, Stateflow might throw an unclear error message. For example, considering the following enumeration: classdef MyEnum < Simulink.IntEnumType   enumeration      A(0)      B(1)   endend Using the undefined enumerator MyEnum.C will cause the following error to be thrown: Parsing failed for machine: [...] If you use MATLAB as the Action Language, you receive a proper error message: 'C' is not a valid enumeration name of 'MyEnum'.

Summary: Slow copying for large subsystems into Simulink models that store RMI data externally.
Description: When a Simulink model is configured to store RMI data externally,  copying a large subsystem into the model can take a long time. The copying time can increase with every subsequent copy operation. This can happen even if the copied subsystem does not contain RMI links.
Workaround: Locate your MATLAB installation directory. For example: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2013b.Back up the original objCopy.p file under toolbox/slvnv/reqmgt/+rmidata/.To patch R2013b, use the attached toolbox_patch_G1156691_R2013b.zip.To patch R2014a or R2014b, use the attached toolbox_patch_G1156691_R2014ab.zip.Unzip the correct attachment under your MATLAB installation directory.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Library Browser filtering can hide libraries that were not intended to be hidden by the filter.
Description: Using the Library Browser customization API for filtering to hide a library causes the libraries that come after them to be hidden as well.
Workaround: In an sl_customization.m file, set the filter state to 'Disabled' (instead of 'Hidden'). All of the other libraries are visible, enabled, and usable. However, the desired library is disabled and its blocks are unusable from the LibraryBrowser.

Summary: Incorrect spring torque computation in revolute joint primitives
Description: The internal spring torque computed at a revolute joint primitive during simulation may be incorrect if: The joint is in a kinematic loopˇŞa closed circuit of rigid bodies and joints that begins and ends at the same frame. SimMechanics selects the joint as a cut jointˇŞone that is replaced by an equivalent constraint to enable the solution of the kinematic loop. The revolute primitive angle is outside the range [-pi, pi] rad.   When incorrect, the spring torque differs from its correct value by a multiple of (2*pi rad)*k , where k  is the spring stiffness specified in the joint block dialog box. The torque may also show discontinuities at certain threshold angles.
Workaround: You can work around this bug by computing the revolute primitive spring torque explicitly using Simulink blocks. To do this:In the Revolute Primitive area of the joint block dialog box, set the Internal Mechanics > Spring Stiffness parameter to 0.Set the Actuation > Torque parameter to Provided by Input.Select the Sensing > Position check box.Connect the position sensing port to the input port of a PS Gain block. You can drag this block from the Simscape > Physical Signals > Functions library.Connect the output port of the Gain block to the torque actuation input port of the joint block.In the PS Gain block dialog box, set the Gain parameter to -k, where k is the desired spring constant.In place of the PS Gain block, you can use a Simulink Gain block. However, in this case you must convert the position sensing signal to a Simulink signal using a PS-Simulink Converter block. You must also convert the Simulink Gain output signal to a physical signal using a Simulink-PS Converter block.

Summary: MATLAB may crash while attempting to resolve an unresolved library link
Description: MATLAB may crash if, while the Model Explorer is open, you open the parameter dialog box of an Unresolved Link block, enter the source block path, and click OK .
Workaround: Close Model Explorer before entering the source block path in the parameter dialog box of an Unresolved Link block.

Summary: Simulink.MDLInfo.getMetadata returns an empty array for libraries
Description: Simulink.MDLInfo.getMetadata  always returns an empty array for library models, even if the library contains metadata.
Workaround: Load the library into memory and then use get_param to access the metadata:>> load_system('libname')  >> get_param('libname','Metadata')

Summary: CAN Transmit block executes only once per model execution
Description: CAN Transmit block will only execute one time per model execution, and not each time it is triggered or enabled, when all of the following conditions exist: Inline Parameters  optimization option is selected The block has a constant input (Inf) sample time signal The block is part of a conditional subsystem with a Trigger or Enable port, such as a Function-Call Subsystem, Triggered Subsystem, Enabled Subsystem, or Enabled and Triggered Subsystem

Summary: Models containing Simscape blocks in subsystem variants may cause MATLAB to terminate unexpectedly
Description: Models containing Simscape blocks inside subsystem variants sometimes cause MATLAB to terminate unexpectedly without generating a crash log. Physical connection lines or physical signals have to cross the subsystem boundaries in order for the bug to be triggered.
Workaround: You can visit Bug Report ID 1184018 to download a patch that addresses this and other issues in R2014b.

Summary: Images in Microsoft Word header or footer template do not appear in generated report
Description: In a Word header or footer template file that uses DOM API?DOCXHeader ?and?DOCXFooter ?classes to define fixed content and holes for generated content, images included in the template do not appear in the generated report. The following script illustrates the problem: ? ?import mlreportgen.dom.*?? ?% Create the image figure;? ?imagesc(magic(3));? ?saveas(gcf,'magic.jpg');?? ?% Create a template with a table that contains a logo and? ?% a company name? ?template = Template('MyTemplate','docx');? ?open(template);? ?image = Image('magic.jpg');? ?image.Width = '0.50in';? ?image.Height = '0.30in';? ?table = Table({image,' ACME Incorporation'});? ?append(template,table);? ?close(template);?? ?% Create a document from default template? ?% and add a header from a separate template? ?d = Document('MyDocument','docx');? ?open(d);?? ?% Set the header of current section? ?% created from a separate template? ?header = DOCXPageHeader('first', 'MyTemplate');? ?open(header);? ?d.CurrentDOCXSection.PageHeaders = header;?? ?close(d);? ?rptview(d.OutputPath)
Workaround: To include images in a header or footer template:Create and store the header or footer template in the document part template library in a template.Specify the header or footer template name in the constructor for the DOCXHeader or DOCXFooter object.For example, if the MyWorkAroundTemplate.dotx contains a document part template library entry called MyHeader with the header template, then the workaround is:? ?import mlreportgen.dom.*? ?% Create a document from default template? ?% and add a header from a separate template? ?d = Document('MyDocument','docx');? ?open(d);? ?% Set the header of current section? ?% created from a separate template? ?% with a document part template library entry 'MyHeader'? ?header = DOCXPageHeader('first','MyWorkAroundTemplate','MyHeader');? ?open(header);? ?d.CurrentDOCXSection.PageHeaders = header;? ?close(d);? ?rptview(d.OutputPath);?

Summary: Selecting Display > Interface in the Simulink Editor might cause a crash
Description: In the Simulink Editor, if you select Display > Interface  for a model that previously updated or simulated with an error, MATLAB might crash.
Workaround: To work around this issue, do not select Display > Interface in the Simulink Editor for a model that previously updated or simulated with an error.

Summary: Can lose run-time tunability of Frequency parameter in code generated for Signal Generator block
Description: The Frequency  parameter in code generated for a Signal Generator block is not tunable at run-time if you configure its parameters as follows: Wave form  parameter is set to sine  Units  parameter is set to Hertz  Frequency  parameter value is stored as a tunable parameter  Under these conditions, the code generator returns the following warning: Reducing run-time parameter 'Frequency' of '<path to block>' to its numerical equivalent because the parameter is a transformation of one or more dialog parameters. You will be unable to interface to the run-time parameter in the generated code even though the corresponding block parameters map to tunable variables.
Workaround: To realize tunability in the Frequency parameter, specify its value with Units set to rad/sec.

Summary: Simulation Data Inspector shows incorrect comparison plots when comparing double to integer
Description: When comparing a baseline signal with data type double  to a signal of type int32  , the difference and tolerance signals might appear incorrect in the Simulation Data Inspector plot. The comparison result (within tolerance or out of tolerance) in the Comparisons  pane shows the correct result.
Workaround: In the Compare tab, switch the order of the run comparison and make the integer signal the Baseline and the double signal the Compare To. The results will appear correct.

Summary: Simulation menu not visible when editing a Stateflow library chart
Description: When you view the contents of a Stateflow Chart block in the model editor of a library, the Simulation  menu is missing. As a result, you cannot set breakpoints or set a code target for the chart.
Workaround: To get the code menu to appear, type: sl_refresh_customizationsAlternatively, you can save the model with the Chart Editor showing and reopen the model.

Summary: Simulink Real-Time Explorer shows error message when starting: "Object reference not set to an instance of an object"
Description: Upon starting the Simulink Real-Time Explorer, its main window opens with the following error message: "Object reference not set to an instance of an object". This problem is caused by a Simulink Real-Time Explorer bug that appears when the .NET framework 4.0 is installed on the Windows computer. This problem is not an issue when .NET framework 4.5 or higher is installed.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation when model contains reusable function call subsystems
Description: When you use multiple masked instances of a reusable function-call subsystem in the same model, generated code might incorrectly pass parameters to the reusable function. This issue occurs in the generated code when a model meets all of these conditions: The model configuration parameter InlineParameters  is cleared The model uses multiple masked instances of a reusable function-call subsystem At least one mask parameter has a different value for each function-call subsystem The mask parameters do not reference any variables The model uses multiple caller blocks each contained in a separate reusable subsystem

Summary: First few samples may not be uploaded in "one-click" external mode simulation
Description: In a "one-click" external mode simulation (where clicking the Play  button builds the model and starts the model simulation), the software might not upload the first few samples of the simulation from the target hardware to the host computer.
Workaround: To open the file TargetHook.m, in the Command Window, run the command edit coder.oneclick.TargetHook.In the file, find the method extModeConnect.At the end of the method, add the line pause(1);Save the file.To rerun the simulation, click the Play button.

Summary: The Code Generation > AUTOSAR Code Generation pane and custom target panes under Code Generation might not visible in the Configuration Parameters dialog box
Description: The Code Generation > AUTOSAR Code Generation  pane and custom target panes under Code Generation  are not visible in the Configuration Parameters dialog box when loaded from a a MAT-file or a protected model. These panes are defined in TLC code using the rtwoptions  array in a system target file. While the parameters defined in the system target file are not visible in the Configuration Parameters dialog box, they are still available and can be accessed from the command line using the the get_param  and set_param  functions. The panes are visible when loading a configuration set from a MAT-file created with a software version earlier than R2014a, but the panes might not be updated or work correctly if the system target file has changed since the MAT-file was generated.

Summary: Error incorrectly reported during simulation of AUTOSAR model containing Simulink function
Description: If you simulate a model configured for AUTOSAR that contains a Simulink function, an error might be incorrectly reported if all of the following conditions are true: The property canBeInvokedConcurrently  is set to true  for the AUTOSAR runnable corresponding to the Simulink function. The Simulink function contains a Gain, Product, or Sum block. The Gain, Product, or Sum block operates on a built-in data type (that is, not a fixed-point data type). Either or both of the model configuration parameters Diagnostics > Data Validity > Wrap on overflow  and Diagnostics > Data Validity > Saturate on overflow  is set to warning .  Under these conditions, simulation might incorrectly generate an error similar to the following: Function 'ChangeParam' may not be invoked concurrently because it contains block 'model /Simulink Function/Gain' which may access global data through its dwork vectors.
Workaround: To work around this bug, perform either of the following actions:Set the canBeInvokedConcurrently property of the AUTOSAR runnable corresponding to the Simulink function to false. Note that the canBeInvokedConcurrently property pertains to generated code. Therefore, you can set the property to false for simulation and revert it to true for code generation, and achieve the intended functionality of the property.Change the settings of the model configuration parameters Diagnostics > Data Validity > Wrap on overflow and Diagnostics > Data Validity > Saturate on overflow to none or error. Specifying error retains the data validity checks.

Summary: Model Reference RTW target sometimes fails to honor the Single output/update function option
Description: When you select the Configuration Parameter > Code Generation > Interface > Code interface > Single output/update function  parameter, the Model Reference RTW target can fail to honor the setting when both of these conditions exist:  A block is driven by a root input. That block is also driven by another block that executes before it.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, use the block contect menu Properties > Priority setting to adjust the sorted order on the affected blocks so that no block being driven by a root input is also driven by another block that executes before it.

Summary: Very long or very large text is not displayed or printed possibly causing MATLAB to crash
Description: Very long or large text might not display on the screen or in printed output. In some cases, the text might be replaced with a gray box or cause MATLAB to crash. You might also encounter one of these issues when printing text at high DPI values. The threshold at which the display of the text becomes problematic varies based on the capabilities of a particular system.
Workaround: Possible workarounds include the following:* Shorten the length of very long text strings.* Reduce the height of very large text strings.* Reduce the DPI when printing.* Use hardware OpenGL. Hardware OpenGL typically supports a larger maximum texture size than software OpenGL, which allows MATLAB to display larger text strings.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation may result for S-function block
Description: The code generator may produce incorrect code when the following conditions exist: A model contains at least one S-function block that connects to the root outport. In the Code Generation  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Target Language  is C , and in the Model Interface dialog box, the Function specification  is Model specific C prototypes . In the Code Generation  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Target Language  is C++ , and in the Class Interface dialog box, the Function specification  is I/O arguments step function .  ?
Workaround: If the Target Language is C, set the Function specification to Default model initialize and step functions. If the Target Language is C++, set the Function specification to Void-void step method.

Summary: In a deployed application, a main MATLAB function calling dependent MATLAB functions contained in its private folder fails
Description: In a deployed application, when a main MATLAB function in a particular folder calls dependent MATLAB functions contained in its private folder, you get an error saying undefined function. This only happens when compiling the main MATLAB function outside the current folder.?
Workaround: There is no workaround for this issue.

Summary: Simulink.CoderInfo objects might incorrectly load from MAT-file?
Description: If you load a MAT-file that contains Simulink data objects before you launch Simulink, the associated Simulink.CoderInfo  objects might load in a default state and discard your customizations. Simulink data objects are defined by the classes and subclasses of Simulink.Parameter  and Simulink.Signal . The CoderInfo  property of a data object stores a Simulink.CoderInfo  object.
Workaround: Call load_simulink at the MATLAB command prompt before you load MAT-files that contain Simulink data objects.

Summary: Error in Requirements Report generation:  Invalid Simulink object name: ...../<!DOCTYPE HTML PUBLIC ...
Description: When generating a Requirements Report for a Simulink diagram that has RichText Annotations, Report Generator error may throw an error: Invalid Simulink object name: traceability_color_bug/
Workaround: Unzip the attached patch into your <MATLAB_INST>/toolbox/ folder.Rehash toolboxcache and restart MATLAB.?

Summary: Crash during code generation for certain cases of indexing expressions that multiply noninteger and constant values
Description: In some cases, when a matrix is indexed with an expression that multiplies a noninteger (enumeration or boolean) value and a constant, code generation can fail with an assertion like: IndexInfo.cpp:504 assert failed NumericType::isa(src->type())  ?
Workaround: In places where matrices are accessed with boolean or enumeration types, insert a cast to a numeric type such as int32.For example, consider the following MATLAB? code:index = true;A(index) = 1.0;Replace  A(index) = 1.0;withA(int32(index)) = 1.0;

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation with Reusable custom storage class (CSC) specification at the Simulink Function boundary.
Description: The generated code might be incorrect if one of the following conditions is true: The signal at the input or the output of a Simulink Function block has a Reusable  CSC specification. The signal at the input or the output of a model reference block has a Reusable  CSC specification and the referenced model contains a Simulink Function block.
Workaround: Remove the Reusable CSC specification from the boundary of a Simulink Function block or from the model reference block containing a Simulink Function block.?

Summary: Generating code for a model that contains reusable subsystems can cause crash
Description: If a model contains multiple instances of a reusable subsystem, building the model can cause MATLAB to crash. MATLAB crashes under the following specific conditions. 1. The reusable subsystem contains a masked virtual subsystem. 2. The only difference between the instances of the reusable subsystem is the value of a mask parameter defined in the underlying masked virtual subsystems. 3. For at least one of the instances of the reusable subsystem, the value of the mask parameter references one or more variables. For another instance, the mask parameter does not reference any variables. 4. The model's InlineParameters setting is OFF.

Summary: Derived range analysis errors for models that contain?enumerations derived from built-in MATLAB classes
Description: Derived range analysis generates the following error when analyzing a model that contains an?enumeration derived from built-in MATLAB classes:? "Min/Max derivation failed to initialize: 'model' is incompatible for design error detection with Fixed-Point Tool range derivation."?

Summary: Fixed-Point Tool disregards Min and Max property of Simulink.Parameter objects
Description: When Fixed-Point Tool proposes data types for a model that utilizes Simulink.Parameter  objects, it only takes the value of parameter object into account, disregarding any range information specified by the Min and Max properties of the parameter objects. Therefore, the proposed data type might not be able to represent the full range of acceptable values.  As a result, if the value of the parameter object is changed, the model might not update successfully, even though the new value is within the original specified range of the parameter object. You are likely to encounter such a situation if the parameter object is used as a tunable variable.

Summary: Undocumented method getDataScope for enumeration classes
Description: Starting in R2011a, when you define an enumeration class in a script file, you can specify that the code that you generate from a model import the enumerated type definition from your own custom header file. In the enumeration class definition, implement the static method getDataScope . The documentation in these releases does not include getDataScope : R2013a R2013b R2014a R2014b  ?
Workaround: If you use any of the affected releases, view the documentation for getDataScope in the attached PDF.

Summary: Some NI DAQ devices do not work with Data Acqusition Toolbox and NI-DAQmx driver version 9.9 or higher
Description: Some NI DAQ devices (such as NI PCI-6251 or NI PCIe-6321) do not work with Session interface when Data Acqusition Toolbox is used with NI-DAQmx driver versions 9.9, 14.0, 14.1, or higher. The following error might occur: NI Error -89120:Source terminal to be routed could not be found on the device.  Make sure the terminal name is valid for the specified device. Refer to Measurement & Automation Explorer for valid terminal names.  Property: DAQmx_RefClk_SrcSource Device: Dev1Source Terminal: PXI_CLK10  Task Name: _unnamedTask<2A>  Status Code: -89120  When displaying device information, NI DAQ devices will also incorrectly appear as PXI modules in a PXI chassis with invalid chassis ans slot numbers: This module is in slot 4294967295 of the PXI Chassis 4294967295.
Workaround: Revert to an older version of the NI-DAQmx driver.

Summary: Error inserting block using smart editing quick insert
Description: Inserting a block with a trailing space in its name using the smart editing quick insert cue results in an error:  "The selected library block no longer exists." This affects some built-in blocks, as well as any custom libraries added to the Library Browser that contain trailing spaces in the block names.
Workaround: Insert these blocks by dragging them into the model from the Library Browser instead.

Summary: Beeping when the Variables editor is open
Description: Error beeps occur when working with the Variables editor, or while the Variables editor is open. This happens in the following situations. A variable that is open in the Variables editor is renamed. The type of a variable that is open in the Variables editor is changed. An object that has been deleted is opened in the Variables editor.
Workaround: Close the Variables editor, and reopen it only for valid variables in the current workspace.

Summary: The ECI to LLA and LLA to ECI block time increment port does not update for different time scales
Description: The ECI to LLA and LLA to ECI block time increment port does not update for different time scales (day, hour, minutes, and seconds). When you change the Time increment  parameter from Day  to another option (Hour , Min , or Sec ), the block output does not reflect that change. The block continues to treat the input to the time increment port as days.

Summary: 32-bit and 64-bit measurements are not handled properly via XCP
Description: The numerical handling of 32-bit and 64-bit measurements as defined in an A2L file was not accurate in the MATLAB interface for XCP. This could result in errors or incorrect calculation of measurement values when reading as a single value or in a data acquisition session.

Summary: MATLAB can crash when accessing elements of a DOM object array
Description: MATLAB can crash when trying to access an element of a DOM object array that has not been explicitly defined as a DOM object.  For example: import mlreportgen.dom.*;oa(3) = Text('hello');tag = oa(2).Tag;  In the code above, the second element of the DOM object array has not been explicitly defined as a DOM object
Workaround: Explicitly assign a DOM object to the array element before accessing it. For example, the following commands avoid the crash:import mlreportgen.dom.*;oa(1) = Paragraph('Hi');oa(2) = Table(magic(5));oa(3) = Text('hello');tag = oa(2).Tag;

Summary: Simulink Preference File format for new models and libraries reverts to slx when you restart Simulink
Description: If you set the Simulink Preference for File format for new models and libraries  to mdl , the value does not persist between Simulink sessions. Every time you restart Simulink, the preference reverts to slx .
Workaround: To work around this problem, programmatically set your preference to mdl every time Simulink starts. Add this line either to a file called startup.m in the folder where you start MATLAB, or to the simulinkrc.m file in your Simulink installation: set_param(0,'ModelFileFormat','mdl')

Summary: Importing spreadsheet data to Root Import Mapping Tool can cause MATLAB to stop responding
Description: Importing spreadsheet data to Root Import Mapping Tool can cause MATLAB to stop responding.

Summary: Unclear error message from optimset and/or inadvertent Optimization Toolbox? license checkout
Description: If you misspell an option name in optimset  and do not have an Optimization Toolbox license, the returned error message can be confusing: Undefined function or variable 'optimoptions'.  If you misspell an option name in optimset  and have an Optimization Toolbox license, MATLAB?  erroneously checks out your toolbox license, but the error message is informative: Unrecognized parameter name 'Invalid_Option_Name'.  If you have a license and try to use optimset  to set an optimoptions -only option, MATLAB erroneously checks out your license and returns this error message: Option 'Option_Name' is not supported by OPTIMSET. Use OPTIMOPTIONS instead.  Finally, if you have a shared license that is currently in use, and you call optimset  with a typo or with an optimoptions -only option, then MATLAB erroneously tries to check out the license and issues this error message: Error using optimoptioncheckfield[value,validvalue] = validateopts_UseParallel(value,false,true)License checkout failed.License Manager Error -5Cannot find a license for Optimization_Toolbox The reason for this behavior is that when MATLAB does not recognize an option name for optimset , it calls optimoptions  as a fallback. This either checks out a license (when you have a license) or results in an error (when you do not have a license).
Workaround: If you do not have an Optimization Toolbox license and simply misspelled an option name, the easiest workaround is to fix the misspelling. Alternatively, to avoid the confusing error message, you can install the attached file.If you have a license and want to avoid inadvertent check-out, install the attached file. optimoptions always checks out the license, so this check-out is mandatory when using an optimoptions-only option.?Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Summary: mapreduce fails when adding an empty string key and empty value using addmulti
Description: mapreduce  fails when adding an empty string key and empty value using addmulti . An error is returned when adding an empty string key and an empty cell value to the KeyValueStore  using addmulti , either in a map function or in a reduce function. For example,ds = datastore('airlinesmall.csv', 'TreatAsMissing', 'NA');mapreducer(0);myMapper = @(~, ~, output) addmulti(output, '', {});myReducer = @(~, ~, output) addmulti(output, '', {});mapreduce(ds, myMapper, myReducer) Error using mapreduce (line 100)Map call #1: Unable to apply the function @(~,~,output)addmulti(output,'',{}) (line 0).Attempted to access input(1); index out of bounds because numel(input)=0.
Workaround: Use workaround and attachment on a related bug report 1129923:https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/1129923

Summary: Simulation Data Inspector issues with logging and run names containing double quotes
Description: If a run name in Simulation Data Inspector contains a double quote, you cannot select, expand, or collapse rows in the table. If a logging name contains a double quote, the signal list appears empty.

Summary: MATLAB will crash when calling the axes function with a handle to a deleted axes object
Description: Using axes  with a single input being the handle to a deleted axes object will cause MATLAB to crash.
Workaround: Place the axes function found in the attached ZIP file in a folder at the top of your MATLAB Search Path. This new function will shadow the original axes function to check for deleted axes objects.Note that you may receive a warning saying the following:Function axes has the same name as a MATLAB builtin. We suggest you rename the function to avoid a potential name conflictTo prevent this type of warnings, execute the following command:>> warning('off','MATLAB:dispatcher:nameConflict')

Summary: Using saveAs to save a configuration reference does not save data and does not result in a warning message
Description: If you use the saveAs  method to save a Simulink.ConfigSetRef  object, the data is not saved and you do not see a warning message that the method cannot be used to save a configuration reference object.
Workaround: Test your object to see if it is a configuration reference using the following:isa(csr, 'Simulink.ConfigSetRef')If your object is a configuration reference and you want to save the object, you must first get the referenced configuration object. Then you can use the saveAs method to save the data. For example:cs = getRefConfigSet(csr)saveAs(cs, fileName)

Summary: Signal appears to change amplitude while zooming in the Simulation Data Inspector
Description: While zooming in the time direction, the amplitude of the signal in the Simulation Data Inspector plot might appear to change.
Workaround: In the Simulation Data Inspector toolstrip, click Send to Figure and compare the MATLAB figure with the Simulation Data Inspector plot. The MATLAB figure and data exported to the base workspace will be correct.

Summary: Crash during code generation for certain cases of matrix indexing in nested for-loops
Description: For certain cases of matrix indexing in nested for -loops, code generation fails with the following assertion: In R2014a:  Assertion at dom_core/analysis/loopAnalysis/Indexing.cpp line 753:!(s1c && s2c) || s1c->size() == s2c->size()  In R2014b:  Assertion at base_core/expr/indexExprAnalysis/IndexInfo.cpp line 564:   !(s1c && s2c) || s1c->size() == s2c->size()  This assertion can occur when all of the following conditions are true: A function contains a nested loop or a loop that contains a vector expression. In the inner loop or vector expression, the function reads an array. The array read uses a scalar index to access one dimension and a vector or array of indices to access another dimension.  The function writes to the array.  An example of code that can trigger the assertion is: function z = func(A)for k = 1:3    rows = [1:(k-1),(k+1):3];    A(rows,k:4) = A(rows,k:4) - A(rows,k)*A(k,k:4);endz = A(1);end ?
Workaround: If you encounter this problem on a regular basis, and need technical assistance regarding the problem, contact MathWorks technical support: https://www.mathworks.com/support/service_requests/contact_support.do

Summary: Possible crash during code generation for handle classes containing both value class and handle class properties
Description: During code generation, MATLAB? can crash if the MATLAB code contains a handle class whose properties include both: A value class object, AND A handle class object.

Summary: Relational comparisons involving datetime or duration arrays can sometimes produce unexpected errors
Description: Unexpected errors can occur when performing relational comparisons involving datetime  arrays that contain Not-a-Time (NaT) values, or duration  arrays that contain NaN values. For example, the following commands are not expected to produce errors: >> d = datetime(2014,9,[1 2 NaN]) d =    01-Sep-2014   02-Sep-2014   NaT>> d > datetime('now')Index exceeds matrix dimensions. >> d = hours([1 2 NaN]) d =       1 hr     2 hrs   NaN hrs>> d > hours(0)  Index exceeds matrix dimensions.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Summary: Generated code fails to compile if Stateflow? chart uses an expression as an argument in a call to custom code
Description: Embedded Coder? generates code that uses an undeclared or undefined variable named unknown  if both: A C-action language Stateflow chart uses an expression as an argument in a call to custom code, for example: my_hand_code_function( a + b )  Configuration parameter CastingMode  is set to Standards   The generated code will be similiar to my_hand_code_function(unknown)  . unknown  is not declared, defined, or assigned,  resulting in an "undeclared identifier" error during compilation. ?
Workaround: Consider using temporary variables to evaluate the expression before the function call. For example, change:my_hand_code_function( a + b );to:temp = a + b;my_hand_code_function( temp );

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: For Each subsystem can generate incorrect code
Description: The code generator can generate incorrect code when the the following conditions exist: A model contains two or more For Each subsystems that are identical except for the number of iterations. One or more of the For Each subsystems iterates once. All of the For Each subsystems take matrix input data (that is, none of them takes scalar input data). For all of the For Each subsystems, Code Generation > Function packaging  is set to Auto  or Reusable function .  The attached example model mForEachBug.zip meets all the above conditions, so it generates incorrect code.
Workaround: The workaround is to change Function Packaging to Inline or Nonreusable function.

Summary: Copy operation on custom code replacement table entry can result in incomplete copy
Description: A copy operation on a custom code replacement table entry that you create, using one of the following methods, can result in an incomplete copy: In the Code Replacement Tool (crtool), click File > New entry > Customization entry . Derive the entry from the class RTW.TflCOperationEntryML  or RTW.TflCFunctionEntryML .  Examples of when a copy operation can occur: In the Code Replacement Tool, you use Edit > Copy  to copy a table or table entries. In the Code Replacement Tool, you open an existing code replacement table and use  File >  Save table  to save changes to a table  Because such entries implement custom match behavior, unexpected or incorrect replacements can occur in the generated code.
Workaround: There are no workarounds. However, use the attached script to determine if your code replacement library contains custom code replacement table entries. For example, to get a list of custom code replacement table entries in My CRL Lib, download and extract the attached .zip file to a folder in your search path and execute the following command: customCrls = getCustomCodeReplacementEntries('My CRL Lib')Change log:May 4, 2015: attachment_1138022_9b-through-15b_2015-05-04.zip: initial version.

Summary: Opening the Block Parameters dialog box for the Bus Creator block can crash MATLAB
Description: When you open the Block Parameters dialog box for a Bus Creator block, MATLAB can crash when all of these conditions apply: The Bus Creator block specifies a bus object. Workaround: Select the affected Bus Creator block and, at the MATLAB command prompt, enter this command:set_param(gcb,'InheritFromInputs','on')

Summary: Reducing the width of MuPAD Notebook window can cause MATLAB to freeze or crash
Description: Depending on your hardware and software configuration, MATLAB? can freeze or crash when you reduce the width of a MuPAD? Notebook window. This is more likely to happen if you have a 3D plot that was activated (clicked on) at least once during the current session. Enlarging the width of a MuPAD Notebook window or changing its height does not cause MATLAB to freeze or crash.

Summary: Model parameter StrictBusMsg cannot be changed in the model's post-load function
Description: The model parameter StrictBusMsg  cannot be changed by using the set_param  function in the model's PostLoadFcn  callback. This callback is defined in the Model Properties dialog box, in the Callbacks  tab. For example, consider the following function in a post-load function: set_param(bdroot,'StrictBusMsg', 'ErrorOnBusTreatedAsVector'); In this case, the parameter remains unchanged.
Workaround: Set the parameter StrictBusMsg by getting the configuration set object for the model. Then call set_param on the object to change the model parameter. For example:set_param(getActiveConfigSet(bdroot),'StrictBusMsg', 'ErrorOnBusTreatedAsVector');

Summary: Model Explorer imports variables k or vars with wrong value
Description: If you use the Model Explorer's Import capability to load variables named k  or vars  from a MAT-file or MATLAB file, the values of these variables may not be imported correctly.
Workaround: To load variables named k or vars into the base workspace, use the MATLAB command prompt to load the MAT-file or run the MATLAB script.To load variables named k or vars into a model workspace, optionally:Use the Model Explorer to set the data source of the model workspace to the target fileUse the MATLAB command prompt to load the variables into the base workspace and then use the Model Explorer to move them into the model workspace?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation for MATLAB? function with multiple outputs when passing a global to a function that reads the same global
Description: Simulink? Coder? can generate incorrect output code when MATLAB function A calls MATLAB function B and the following conditions are met: Function B has multiple outputs Global variable g  is passed as a parameter to function B Global variable g  receives a value returned from function B  For example, consider the following MATLAB code: function [o1,o2] = outer(u3)   global g;   [u3(g),g] = nested(g);   o1 = u3;   o2 = g;   end   function [x,a] = nested(a)   coder.inline('never');   global g; x = g;   a = a + 1;   g = g + a;   end   The compiler can generate code that incorrectly reads the value of g  prior to the function call. The code uses the cached value to index into u3 . For correctly generated code, the code indexes into u3  using the value of g  after the function call.
Workaround: Avoid passing a global by name as a parameter to a function that directly or indirectly reads the same global by name.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation when global variables are passed as parameters to MATLAB? functions that read the same global variable?
Description: When generating code for models that incorporate embedded MATLAB code, Simulink? Coder? can generate incorrect code when a global variable is passed to a function that accesses the same global variable by name. For example, consider the following MATLAB code: function [o] = outer()    global g;     g = nested(g);     o = g;     end     function a = nested(a)     coder.inline('never');     global g;     a.f1 = a.f1 + 1;     a.f1 = a.f1 + g.f1;     end     In the code generated, the compiler incorrectly passes g  by reference to the function nested . When you execute the code, the value assigned to a.f1  is incorrect.
Workaround: Avoid passing global structures and arrays as parameters to functions that access the global structure by name.

Summary: Packaging an app may result in an error if you include the folder that contains the main file
Description: When packaging an app, if you include the folder that contains the main file as a "Shared Resource or Helper File" you may receive the following error message: Packaging the app...Not enough input arguments.
Workaround: Instead of choosing to include the containing folder as the Shared Resource, navigate into the folder and choose to include all of the files contained in the folder.

Summary: Unnecessary reference and dereference generated when child model has C++ class interface with an input or output passed by reference
Description: Generated code for a parent model call into a child model might contain an unnecessary reference and dereference if both of the following conditions are met: The parent model and child model both are configured to generate a C++ class interface. At least one inport or outport of the child model is configured to be passed by reference. (That is, in the Configure C++ class interface dialog box, an inport or outport has the Category  parameter set to Reference .)  Here is an example of a parent model call to a child model step function with unnecessary reference and dereference: ChildObj.step(*(&arg_In2), (int32_T(&)[2])(*(&rtb_gain[0]));

Summary: MATLAB job scheduler fails to attach files to job at security level 3
Description: The MATLAB job scheduler can fail to correctly attach files to a job at security level 3. This occurs only when a previous job with attached files, submitted by a different user, has crashed.  This issue can occur on parallel pools with attached files, where the error includes the text: Error using parallel.internal.pool.InteractiveClient/start (line 358) Failed to initialize the interactive session. Error using parallel.internal.pool.InteractiveClient>iThrowIfBadParallelJobStatus (line 726) The interactive communicating job errored with the following message: Error encountered while adding AttachedFiles to MATLAB path.  Subsequent batch jobs that include attached files can also fail. This causes the fetchOutputs  function to throw an error that includes the text: Error encountered while adding AttachedFiles to MATLAB path.

Summary: Stateflow SourceOClock and DestinationOClock transition properties do not work correctly if the transition ends on a subchart boundary
Description: For simple transitions (not supertransitions) that connect to the inside boundary of a subchart, reading or setting the SourceOClock  and DestinationOClock  position properties fails to work correctly.
Workaround: To read or set the SourceOClock or DestinationOClock properties correctly, perform these steps.Right-click the subcharted state and in the context menu, clear the Group & Subchart > Subchart check box.Read or set the SourceOClock or DestinationOClock properties.Right-click the state and select the Group & Subchart > Subchart check box to convert back to a subchart.

Summary: mapreduce on a local cluster can cause memory issues when the input datastore contains many small files
Description: Using mapreduce  with a local cluster on a datastore that contains many small files might exhibit two unexpected behaviors: 1. The MATLAB workers can leak some amount of memory. 2. The client MATLAB can temporarily use a large amount of memory, which can cause paging-to-disk and out-of-memory errors.
Workaround: Use the workaround and attachment for the related bug report 1144305:https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/1144305

If thedestination block is a bus-capable block, ensure that the block configuration and its input signal(s) meet the requirements for bus support. Summary: Code generation for RAM fails with assertion
Description: HDL code generation can fail with an assertion that is similar to:b:\matlab\src\cgir_hdl\pir_transforms\minimizeclockenables.cpp:425:currentDriver  This failure occurs only when all the following conditions are true:The generated HDL uses a single clock, with no timing controller. The design includes RAM blocks from the HDL Operations library. The RAM Architecture  option is set to "Generic RAM without clock enable". The Minimize clock enables  option is enabled.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, change any of the four prerequisite conditions. Of the four conditions, disabling the "Minimize clock enables" option will likely have the least impact on your generated code.

Summary: Certain user MATLAB files are not picked up by dependency analyzer
Description: User MATLAB files with certain names are not picked up by dependency analyzer.  Thus the deployed component may fail with the below error when attempted to run: Undefined function or variable function_name  These names include, but are not limited to: name.misfunction.mbuiltinClassCTOR.mbuiltinClassName.mclassFiles.mclassName.mclassType.mclassUsingBuiltinCTOR.mexistClass.mexistFile.mexisting.mextension.mfindAtDirOnPath.mflatten.mgetDirContents.mgetPrivateFiles.mgetSymbolNames.mhasext.minit_db_table_data.misClassdef.misDirOnPath.misExecutable.misMcode.misStaticMethod.misbuiltin.misfullpath.misfunction.misscript.mlicensed.mmatlabBuiltinClasses.mmatlabFileExists.mmsg2why.mqualifiedName.mqualifyName.mrealpath.mwhichNonBuiltin.mwhichCache.msymCheckList.mw.mw_nonBuiltIn.mpth.m
Workaround: Rename your files to a different name that doesn't conflict with one of the above names.

Summary: PIL or SIL simulation with code profiling on Windows might open external editor and subsequently fail simulation
Description: On a Windows system, simulating a model might unexpectedly open a source file in an external editor (for example, Visual Studio) if the following conditions are met: The simulation mode is Processor-in-the-Loop (PIL) or Software-in-the-Loop (SIL). Code instrumentation is turned on. That is, in the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation > Verification  pane, one of the Code profiling  options is selected. Shared code generation is configured to use a shared location. That is, in the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation > Interface  pane, the Shared code placement  parameter is set to Shared location .  Under these conditions, simulating the model might open an external editor. When you close the editor, the simulation fails with a message similar to the following: Error using coder.make.Builder/coder_diagnose (line 2891) Error(s) encountered while building model "nosine_control_test": ### Failed to generate all binary outputs.

Description: Installing a support package using the Support Package Installer might crash MATLAB. Summary: Error during model update when a Stateflow chart calls a Simulink function with an enum as an input or output argument

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code for some cases of multiple sort calls with different sort orders in MATLAB Function block
Description: The generated code for sort  in a MATLAB function block can sort in the wrong direction when the following conditions are true: The MATLAB function block calls sort  more than once, with the sorting direction set to 'ascend'  for some calls  and to 'descend'  for other calls. All of the other arguments in the sort  calls have identical values. On the Code Generation>Interface  pane, the Shared code placement  parameter is set to Shared location .   For these conditions, Simulink Coder generates code that sorts in only one order (either ascending or descending).  Depending on certain model properties, this error can also occur when the model's Shared code placement  parameter is set to Auto.  ? ? ?
Workaround: Try configuring the model with Shared code placement parameter set to Auto. This workaround might not succeed for all models.

Summary: Passing virtual bus signal into a Stateflow chart with atomic subcharts produces incorrect simulation result and generated code
Description: The simulation result is incorrect and generated code is wrong if all  of the following conditions are true: You feed a virtual bus into a Stateflow chart.  The corresponding Inport block in the Stateflow chart specifies its Data type  as Inherit: Same as Simulink . This Stateflow chart contains an atomic subchart that uses the virtual bus signal.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, use one of these approaches:Explicitly specify a bus object as the data type of the Inport block of the Stateflow chart. So, instead if Inherit: Same as Simulink, the data type would be Bus: X, where X is the bus object name. Convert the virtual bus to a nonvirtual bus by placing a Signal Conversion block at the input port of the Stateflow chart. Set the Signal Conversion block Output parameter is to Nonvirtual bus.

Summary: Loading a model with a configuration reference in a different version of Simulink might result in a warning message about the data dictionary.
Description: Loading a model containing a configuration reference might result in a warning message about the data dictionary if the model was saved in a different version of Simulink. In this case, you will see the following warning: Warning: Parameter "DDName" was not expected here Warning: A standalone configuration set reference does not allow to use data dictionary.
Workaround: In Model Explorer, remove the configuration set from the model then add it back again. When this is completed, resave the model.

Summary: Plot edit mode does not allow you to move or resize graphics objects in figures with normalized units
Description: If you are in plot edit mode for a figure that has a Units  property set to 'normalized' , then you cannot interactively move or resize some graphics objects in the figure. For example, you cannot move or resize axes or uipanel objects. Additionally, if you click the figure background, then the selection handles display in the lower-left corner of the figure instead of around the figure.
Workaround: To interactively move or resize graphics objects in the figure when in plot edit mode, set the Units property to 'pixels'. For example, to change the Units property for the current figure, use set(gcf,'Units','pixels'). To revert back to normalized units after exiting plot edit mode, use set(gcf,'Units','normalized').

Summary: Printed/exported output for figures can have incorrect size or scale if the root object's units are not pixels.
Description: Printed/exported output for figures can have an incorrect size or scale if the Units property of the root object is not set to pixels. For example, this code results in output that is not sized or positioned correctly, and has very thick lines and large text: set(groot,'Units','centimeters');  figure;  plot(1:10);  print(gcf,'my_figure_bad.pdf','-dpdf');
Workaround: Change the Units property of the root object to pixels before printing/saving a figure:old = get(groot,'Units'); set(groot,'Units','Pixels'); print(gcf,'my_figure_good.pdf','-dpdf'); set(groot,'Units',old);

Summary: Model Advisor check 'Identify questionable operations for strict single-precision design' may return incorrect results
Description: If one of the root-level inport or outport blocks in a Simulink model introduce double-precision operations, then the Model Advisor check 'Identify questionable operations for strict single-precision design' may incorrectly report that other root-level inport or outport blocks introduce double-precision operations as well.
Workaround: To work around the issue, please run the attached diagnostic script on your model before running the Model Advisor check. The script will correctly list all the blocks that need to be converted to single-precision, and only those blocks. Once you have completed the conversion, you can run the check. To run the script, enter >> prepareRootIOs('model_name')where model_name is the name of your model.

Summary: MATLAB may crash on selection if you create an annotation when zoomed to 40% or less
Description: MATLAB may crash when you perform the following sequence of actions in Simulink: Set the zoom level of the Editor to 40% or less. Double-click in a blank area to try to create an annotation.  Immediately marquee select that area or click on the annotation to select it.
Workaround: To prevent the crash, take another action in the Editor after creating the annotation but before attempting to select it; for example, move a block or line slightly.You can visit Bug Report ID 1184018 to download a patch that addresses this and other bugs in R2014b.

Summary: Logical Operator (XOR, NXOR, NAND, NOR) block does not error out when it is fed one scalar input of certain data types
Description: When a Logical Operator block acts as XOR, NXOR, NAND or NOR block, it allows one scalar input and behaves like a Data Type Conversion block unless both input and output data types are double or boolean. This behavior is confusing because giving only one input for  XOR, NXOR, NAND or NOR operations is designed to error out. (Note: AND, OR and NOT operators accept one scalar input)

Summary: Model containing function-call subsystem invoked by function-call generators might generate empty model functions?
Description: A model that contains a function-call subsystem that is invoked by a  function-call generator, might generate model functions, such as Start() , which are empty apart from automatically generated comments. The generated code is similar to the following: /* Start for function-call system: '<S1>/Test' */ void Test_Start(void)  {  /* Start for S-Function (fcncallgen): '<S2>/Function-Call Generator' incorporates:    *   Start for SubSystem: '<S1>/Subsystem'   */} ?
Workaround: On the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation > Comments pane, disable comment generation. The absence of comments allows the code generator to optimize away the empty functions.An alternative workaround is to remove any empty, uncalled, or commented-out functions from your TLC files.

Summary: MATLAB crashes while executing intlinprog or linprog.
Description: intlinprog  (R2014a and R2014b) and the linprog  'dual-simplex'  algorithm (R2014b) can crash when trying to factor an LP basis matrix that is singular.

Summary: False positive on MISRA C: 2012 rule 10.3
Description: MISRA C: 2012 rule 10.3 states, "The value of an expression shall not be assigned to an object with a narrower essential type or of a different essential type category ".  If you declare effective Boolean types using typedef , Polyspace may raise a false positive MISRA C:2012 rule 10.3 result. Example:// added boolean and uint8 as effective boolean type in MISRA 2012 using option -boolean-types boolean,uint8 typedef unsigned char boolean;typedef unsigned char uint8;   extern unsigned char b; boolean foo(void) {   boolean a1;   _Bool a2;   a1 = b == (uint8)2; // False positive on a1 assignment - rule 10.3   a2 = b == (uint8)2; // compliant   return (a1 || a2); // False positive on || - rule 10.3   }

Summary: Parallel Computing Toolbox does not use all the processor groups for a local cluster
Description: All the workers of a local cluster can end up using a single processor group on a Windows machine. This problem occurs only if you have a machine with more than 64 cores and you are required to use Windows processor groups. The underlying problem is that some Windows API calls prevent the operating system from correctly affinitizing subprocesses to processor groups.
Workaround: Because the problem is caused by software used by MATLAB graphics, you can avoid it by not initializing graphics at startup or using graphics in MATLAB.Starting MATLAB with the flag -softwareopengl allows you to use all processor groups until you use MATLAB graphics.?

Summary: A commented out Model block can give a 'Model not found' error on simulation in Rapid Accelerator mode
Description: If a model contains a commented out Model block and if the Model block references a model that does not exist, Simulink will give a 'Model not found' error if the model is simulated in Rapid Accelerator mode. The same error message will be displayed if Model blocks are refreshed using the top menu option Diagram -> Refresh Blocks.
Workaround: Change the commented out Model blocks to reference models that exist.

Summary: Bus Creator block gets a truncated signal name if the propagated signal label has a ">" in the name
Description: If there is a propagated signal label that includes a <  at the beginning  or a >  in the name, such as <Sol=>Avion> , the name displayed in down stream Bus Creator block is truncated as Sol=.
Workaround: Do not use < as the first character of a signal name, and do not use > in a signal name.

When a second network interface is active (for example, VPN or Wireless), the local cluster functionality of Parallel Computing Toolbox can fail with a Java exception. So, running commands like parpool('local')  or parpool  (with local as the default profile) with the Wireless or VPN turned on, might exhibit this behaviour. Summary: Stateflow charts and MATLAB Function blocks that use enumerations error out incorrectly when using non-default hardware settings
Description: When simulating a model that contains a Stateflow chart, MATLAB Function, or Truth Table block that uses enumerations, an incorrect type mismatch error message sometimes aborts simulation prematurely.  The error message has the form  Type mismatch: EnumType ~= EnumType even though both the left hand side and the right hand side of the expression that the message points to are of the same type. This bug occurs when both of the following conditions are true: The hardware implementation in the model configuration parameters defines a size for int that is not 32 bits  The enumeration class inherits from Simulink.IntEnumType
Workaround: There are two workarounds depending on where the enumerated type is defined.If the enumerated type is defined in an M file, then change the base class of the enumerated type from Simulink.IntEnumType to an integral type, such as int16, whose size matches the size of the target integer type specified by the model's hardware settings. So, for instance, if the hardware settings define int to be 32 bits, then choose int32 as the base class for the enumeration type.If the enumerated type is defined in a Simulink Data Dictionary, then navigate to the dictionary using the View > Model Explorer > Data Dictionary menu option. Click on the enum definition, and change the storage type from Native Integer to an integral type that has the same size as the target integer specified by the hardware settings.

Summary: MATLAB crashes on startup or when using "cftool" with AMD?/ATI? Radeon graphics cards
Description: MATLAB R2014b can crash upon start in computers that utilize AMD?/ATI? graphics cards with the driver version 12. In prior releases, this crash can occur when using "cftool" instead. To determine if you are experiencing this issue, the following two steps must be true The resulting crash log "matlab_crash_dump.XXXX" contains the "jogamp" keyword. Refer to the following article to locate the crash log files generated by MATLAB: How do I locate the crash log files generated by MATLAB?  In some instances, the keyword may not be found in the "matlab_crash_dump.XXXX", but will instead be found in the "hs_error_pidXXXX.log" crash log file. Your computer has an AMD?/ATI? graphics card that uses the driver version 12. Refer to the following article to determine which driver version your computer uses: How do I determine the version of my graphics card driver from my MATLAB crash logs?
Workaround: Upgrade your AMD?/ATI? drivers directly from the AMD website to version 14.4 or higher:AMD?/ATI? Driver DownloadThe recommended workflow is to uninstall the previous driver before installing the updated driver; this will make sure the new drivers are installed correctly.Note that in some instances your operating system or computer manufacturer software may say your drivers are up to date, however, the driver version is still lower than 14.4.  You can identify the version of your drivers by inspecting the graphics card driver properties.  ?

Summary: TLC code generation error when generating S-Function block from subsystem
Description: If you use the S-function target (rtwsfcn.tlc ) to generate an S-Function block from a subsystem, the build process generates a TLC error if both of the following conditions are true: A subsystem input is a Data Store Read block or a subsystem output is a Data Store Write block. The corresponding data store memory is not read or written in the subsystem itself.  Under these conditions, the build fails with a TLC error similar to the following: Error: "Unknown SFcnWrapperMode \"none\""Main program:==> [00] $(matlabroot)/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/sfcnlib.tlc:SLibGenDataStoreWrites(986) [01] $(matlabroot)/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonbodlib.tlc:SLibGetBodyOutputFcnCache(2601) [02] $(matlabroot)/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/sfcnlib.tlc:<NONE>(984) [03] $(matlabroot)/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonbodlib.tlc:SLibGetRateGroupedFcnBody(1750) [04] $(matlabroot)/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonbodlib.tlc:SLibGetSystemFcnBodyCacheHelper(1934) [05] $(matlabroot)/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonbodlib.tlc:SLibGetSystemBodyCache(2112) [06] $(matlabroot)/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/formatbody.tlc:SLibFormatBody(126) [07] $(matlabroot)/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/formatwide.tlc:<NONE>(176) The displayed line numbers may vary depending on your MATLAB version.
Workaround: To work around this bug, open the subsystem and insert a Data Store Read block that reads data from the data store memory inside the subsystem, so that the data store memory is accessed at least once.

Summary: AutoSyncDSA cannot be enabled for a session with different device models
Description: AutoSyncDSA  synchronization cannot be enabled for a session  with multiple analog input channels from different DSA device models (such as NI PXI-4461 and NI PXI-4462, or NI PXIe-4497 and NI PXIe-4492). s = daq.createSession('ni');addAnalogInputChannel(s, 'PXI1Slot6', 0, 'Voltage');addAnalogInputChannel(s, 'PXI1Slot8', 0, 'Voltage');  Setting AutoSyncDSA  session  property to true  results in an error: s.AutoSyncDSA = true;  When the AutoSyncDSA property is set to true and the session contains channels from different devices, the EnhancedAliasRejectionEnable property is read only.

Summary: For matrices with repeated eigenvalues, eigs can return different results each call, even when the results should be the same.
Description: By default, eigs  uses a randomly-generated starting vector for each call, so the output is not always exactly reproducible. To get reproducible results, the starting vector can be set manually using the syntax: d = eigs(A, k, sigma, opts) where opts  is a structure with the field v0  containing any vector. This method gives reproducible results because the starting vector v0  is fixed instead of random. However, a bug occurs if the matrix A  has a repeated eigenvalue and the starting vector opts.v0  is an eigenvector of A  with that eigenvalue. In this case, the underlying algorithm does a restart, and the restart uses different random numbers for each call. Resetting the random seed does not solve the problem, because the random numbers for eigs  are generated in a third-party library. This is not a very common case. However, one example where it always happens is when A  is the identity matrix. In that case, A  has one repeated eigenvalue, and any vector is an eigenvector.
Workaround: The output of eigs can be made reproducible by forcing a reload of the third-party library that generates the random numbers on every call. This resets the random seed. This approach can be computationally expensive.Example:clear arpackc;[v,d] = eigs(eye(3), 1, 'sa', struct('v0', ones(3, 1)))

Summary: Vertical eye opening and eye height measurements may be incorrect
Description: The eye diagram analysis commscope.eyediagram  performs vertical eye opening, eye height, and jitter measurements based on the dual-Dirac model. This model assumes that the PDF of the measured data (either eye levels or jitter) is a combination of two deterministic values and a Gaussian distribution. If the data has more than two deterministic values, then the estimated values may be wrong.

Summary: From Workspace block loading of large data in structure format can crash or hang MATLAB
Description: MATLAB can crash or hang when both of these conditions exist: A From Workspace block is loading structure data (with or without a time vector). The signal.values  field of the input structure is an array for which the product of its number of rows, number of columns, and array data type size (in bytes) exceeds the maximum size that your computer platform supports for integers.
Workaround: Consider using multiple From Workspace blocks for loading the data. You can split the structure being loaded into multiple structures and load each of the smaller structures using its own From Workspace block. To combine the data loaded by the multiple From Workspace blocks, you can add a Mux block.

Workaround: To avoid this problem: Turn off all but one network connections on the machine, e,g, turn off wifi.  Start MATLAB and run the comparison. If you still see the problem, turn off all network connections and try again.

Summary: intlinprog and the linprog 'dual-simplex' algorithm can return a solution that does not satisfy bounds (lb and ub).
Description: When intlinprog  (R2014a and R2014b) or the linprog  'dual-simplex'  algorithm (R2014b) solve a problem with the LPPreprocess  (intlinprog ) or Preprocess  (linprog  'dual-simplex' ) option set to its default value, the solution can fail to satisfy some bounds (lb  and ub ). This rare error is due to a fault in an internal LP simplification routine.
Workaround: Disable LP preprocessing by setting the LPPreprocess (intlinprog) or Preprocess (linprog 'dual-simplex') option to 'none' using optimoptions.

Summary: Possible identifier length and uniqueness issues with enumerated types
Description: If you specify that generated enumerated type value names include the class name prefix from the enumerated type definition, the generated names might: Exceed the maximum identifier length that you specified (MaxIdLength  in the code configuration object or Maximum identifier length  in a project). Be the same as another generated identifier.   For example, consider the enumerated type: classdef Colors < int32    enumeration        Red (1)        Green678911234567892123456789312 (2)    end    methods (Static)        function p = addClassNameToEnumNames()            p = true;        end    endend  The generated code can have these issues: Suppose that the maxium identifier length is the default value, 31. The generated identifier for the enumerated value  Green678911234567892123456789312  is Colors_Green678911234567892123456789312  which exceeds  31 characters.  Suppose that the generated name Colors_Red  is the same as another generated identifier. The code generation software might not rename the clashing identifiers to be unique. In this case, the code does not compile. ?

Summary: Creating a protected model with the Generated code content type set to Readable or Obfuscated source code can cause an MD5 error
Description: Creating a protected model with the Generated code content type  set to Readable  or Obfuscated source code  can cause an MD5 error similar to this: An error occurred while computing the MD5 checksum of 'rtGetNaN.h' Java exception occurred: java.io.FileNotFoundException: rtGetNaN.h (The system cannot find the file specified) at java.io.FileInputStream.open(Native Method) at java.io.FileInputStream.(Unknown Source) at com.mathworks.comparisons.util.LocalIOUtils.getFileChecksum(LocalIOUtils.java:127) ? When creating a protected model from the GUI, this error can occur when the Generate Code  checkbox is checked and the Generated code content type  is set to either Obfuscated source cod e or Readable source code . When creating a protected model from the command line, this error can occur when the OutputFormat  parameter is set to AllReferencedHeaders .
Workaround: If this error was encountered when creating the protected model from the GUI, set the Generated code content type to Binaries in the Creation dialog. If this error was encountered when creating the protected model from the command line, set the OutputFormat parameter to CompiledBinaries or MinimalCode.

Summary: Variables in GeoTIFF example shadow xlim and ylim functions

The error issued is a MATLAB:badsubscript  error. (In English, the message is 'Subscript indices must either be real positive integers or logicals.' .)
Workaround: Clear the variables xlim and ylim from your workspace after running the example.

Summary: Code inpection might fail to verify code for model with constant input to a product block.
Description: Simulink Code Inspector might fail to verify code for a model containing a product block if the following conditions are met: The product block has constant inputs. The product block is configured for matrix multiplication. The values of the constant input vectors are all identical.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Mismatch between FFT/IFFT HDL Optimized validOut signal and dataOut signal
Description: When you provide the FFT/IFFT blocks with a non-contiguous validIn signal, the resulting validOut signal doesn't correspond with valid dataOut. After the initial latency period of the FFT/IFFT block, the validOut signal goes high and is held there for a large number of samples. This behavior results in validOut being high on cycles between output frames, when dataOut is not valid. The number of samples that validOut is high is a multiple of the FFT frame size, and also depends on the number of samples that validIn is held low. The expected behavior is that validOut goes high only for each FFT output frame. Output frames on dataOut are contiguous. validOut may be low between output frames depending on the gaps in validIn.

Summary: Files in the current directory are sometimes deleted when exiting MATLAB
Description: Files in the current directory are sometimes deleted when exiting MATLAB?. This issue only occurs with Microsoft Windows as the active Operating System. This issue occurs after the following sequence: Virtual files are copied to the clipboard. This includes:Files copied from a Remote Desktop session Email attachments copied directly from an email client Files copied from within an expanded zip file   A new MATLAB instance is launched or an active MATLAB instance is interacted with, while the files from Step 1 are still on the clipboard. Files with the same name as those added to the clipboard in Step 1 are present in MATLABˇŻs current working directory when MATLAB shuts down.  NOTES: Step 1 in the sequence above can occur at any time before Step 2, so long as the files are on the clipboard when Step 2 occurs. Once Step 1 and Step 2 in the sequence above have occurred, the deletion behavior persists even if the files are removed from the clipboard through another cut/copy action.
Workaround: The issue can be best avoided by using mechanisms such as save as and drag-and-drop to move files from email, zip, and remote desktop applications.PatchInstall the attached patch for either R2014a or R2014b on your PCs. To do that:Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.Download the MATLAB File associated with this Bug Report (installpatch1090500_2015-1-12.zip) to a directory on your computer and unzip the file.Launch MATLAB and navigate to the folder that contains the unzipped file.Run >> installpatch1090500 installRestart MATLAB.ChangelogJanuary 12, 2015: installpatch1090500_2015-1-12.zip: updated workaround, works on R2014a and R2014b.December 12, 2014: attachment_1090500_14b_2014-12-11.zip: updated workaround, works on R2014b only.December 12, 2014: attachment_1090500_14a_2014-12-11.zip: updated workaround, works on R2014a only.October 2, 2014: finish.zip: initial workaround, works on all versions of MATLAB.

Summary: OPC Write block generates misleading error when items not found on server
Description: When an OPC Write block includes an item that cannot be found on the server, running the simulation generates an error dialog with the following message: Error evaluating start method..... Undefined function or variable "ME".
Workaround: This error is generated only when items in the OPC Write block cannot be found on the server. Be sure that the items you are writing to exist on the OPC server, and run the simulation again.
