Description: The Bus Editor reports this error when you set a bus element DataType  property to a bus object that is not listed in the drop-down:  Invalid data type specified. Ensure that the data type specified does not result in a self-referencing cycle.
Workaround: Instead of typing the name of the bus object in the DataType field, select ---Refresh data types---. Then choose the desired bus object from the drop-down and click Apply. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Calling coder.updateBuildInfo in a System Object with boolean parameter values may cause an error
Description: Calling coder.updateBuildInfo  in a System Object with boolean parameter values may cause the following error:  Incorrect arguments have been passed to: RTW.BuildInfo.addLinkObjects Please refer to the help on this method for more information. An example of a call that error out is: coder.updateBuildInfo('addLinkObjects',libName, libPath, '', true, false);

Description: When you use a trackpad or Apple Magic Mouse on Mac OS X, the mouse pointer can vanish when over the Simulink or Stateflow Editor. The pointer reappears when moved outside the Editor window. In some instances, this may also cause MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Code inspection returns "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" for selection on an input signal containing expressions independent of the selection index
Description: Simulink Code Inspector returns "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" when selection on an input signal, using a block such as a Selector block or Multiport Switch block, is performed using an expression containing vector and scalar parts. The scalar parts are independent of the selection index. The resulting generated code performs indexing on only the non-scalar parts of the signal. In this case, Simulink Code Inspector cannot verify the code.
Workaround: Consider performing the selection on the index dependent parts and then apply the scalar parts to the resulting output. For example, if the result of a sum operation on two signals u and k is indexed as (u + k)[i], where u is a vector, k is a constant, and i is a scalar index, the optimized generated code represents this operation as u[i] + k. Consider modeling this operation in Simulink as a selection on u as u[i], and then a sum operation with constant k as u[i] + k.

Summary: Crash on exit with file open in MATLAB Editor on Mac OS X 10.9
Description: On Mac OS X 10.9, MATLAB might crash on exit  or quit  when a file is open in MATLAB Editor.
Workaround: Before quitting MATLAB, close open file(s) in the editor.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Conversion of fi objects in MATLAB with non-zero bias may be incorrect
Description: When converting between fi objects that utilize non-zero bias, the MATLAB interpreted execution may be incorrect. Results from Simulink, Embedded Coder, MATLAB Coder, and fiaccel  are correct. The following MATLAB function shows this issue.  function y = foo(u)  ? u_tmp = fi(u,1,17,12);  ? y = fi(u_tmp,1,16,4,-10);  end   In MATLAB, the function returns an incorrect result.  >> foo(10)  ans =?  ? ? 14  ? ? ? ? ? DataTypeMode: Fixed-point: slope and bias scaling  ? ? ? ? ? ? Signedness: Signed  ? ? ? ? ? ? WordLength: 16  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?Slope: 4  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? Bias: -10  Using fiaccel , the same function produces the correct result.  >> fiaccel foo -args 10?  >> foo_mex(10)  ans =?  ? ? 10  ? ? ? ? ? DataTypeMode: Fixed-point: slope and bias scaling  ? ? ? ? ? ? Signedness: Signed  ? ? ? ? ? ? WordLength: 16  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?Slope: 4  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? Bias: -10?

Summary: Image Acquisition Toolbox linuxvideo adaptor does not work with latest Ubuntu distributions.
Description: Using a camera through the linuxvideo adaptor from Image Acquisition Toolbox on more recent Ubuntu distributions errors. This includes Ubuntu version 13.04+. The camera enumerates correctly and allows you to successfully create a videoinput object, but fails when you try to acquire a frame using getsnapshot or getdata. The code snippet and error is shown below. vi = videoinput('linuxvideo', 1);img = getsnapshot(vi);  Warning: Unable to reset the crop area.  Perhaps the device is in use. Error using imaqdevice/getsnapshot (line 66) Could not connect to the image acquisition device. Device may be in use.

Summary: Simulink produces code generation syntax error when a Level-2 MATLAB S-Function writes string parameters starting with numeric character in WriteRTW method
Description: Simulink produces code generation syntax error when a Level-2 MATLAB S-Function writes string parameters starting with numeric characters in WriteRTW  method. The string is printed without any quotes into the .rtw file causing a code generation syntax error if all of the following are true  for the Level-2 MATLAB S-Function block in the model WriteRTW  method uses WriteRTWParam  function to write a string parameter into .rtw file. At least one of the string parameter values starts  with a numeric character.  For example, suppose a string parameter of a Level-2 MATLAB S-Function is '5 step process'. When this parameter is written to the .rtw file, the quotes around the string are missing as it is treated as a numeric value. This will cause a code generation error.
Workaround: Make sure the first character in the string is an alphabet.In the example mentioned above, change the name from '5 step process' to 'Five step process'.

Summary: MATLAB ldl function can crash when executed with a sparse matrix
Description: The MATLAB ldl  function can crash with a sparse matrix, if 4 output arguments are used. Also, MATLAB backslash (the function mldivide ) can crash when used with a real sparse symmetric matrix which is not positive definite.
Workaround: There is no workaround for the crash in ldl. The crash in mldivide can be avoided by calling spparms('usema57',0). This stops mldivide from using ldl underneath.

Summary: h5read and h5write error for large datasets on Mac OS X
Description: On Mac OS X, the h5read  and h5write  functions error unexpectedly when reading and writing large (>=2GB) datasets. For example, when writing 2GB of data to an HDF5 dataset, this code: % Create an HDF5 file with a 2GB dataset '/dset' h5create('myFile.h5','/dset',[2*1024*1024*1024 /8,1]; h5write('myFile.h5','/dset',a);   % a contains 2GB of double precision numbers Returns the following error message: Error using hdf5lib2 The HDF5 library encountered an unknown error.     Error in H5D.write (line 71) H5ML.hdf5lib2('H5Dwrite', varargin{:});     Error in h5write (line 130) H5D.write(dataset_id,'H5ML_DEFAULT',memspace_id,filespace_id,dxpl,Data); ? When reading a 2GB HDF5 dataset, this code: A = h5read('myFile.h5','/dset'); % '/dset' contains 2GB of double-precision numbers Returns the following error message: Error using hdf5lib2The HDF5 library encountered an error and produced the following stack trace information:     H5FD_sec2_read      file read failed    ˇ­    H5FD_read           driver read request failed    H5F_accum_read      driver read request failed    H5F_block_read      read through metadata accumulator failed    H5D_contig_readvv   block read failed    H5D_select_io       read error    H5D_select_read     read error    H5D_contig_read     contiguous read failed    H5D_read            can't read data    H5Dread             can't read data
Workaround: Write >2GB data to one dataset in chunks, using the h5write function. For example:% Number of doubles in 1GB of memory numDouble = 1024*1024*1024/8;% Create a HDF5 file and dataset to hold 2GB of doublesh5create('myFile.h5','/dset',[numDouble*2 1]); % Fill the first half of the dataset with 1GB of zeros h5write('myFile.h5','/dset',zeros(numDouble,1),[1 1],[numDouble 1]); % Fill the second half of the dataset with 1GB of onesh5write('myFile.h5','/dset',ones(numDouble, 1),[numDouble+1 1], [numDouble 1]);?Read datasets larger than 2GB in separate chunks using the h5read function. Then, concatenate the chunks into a single array. For example:% '/dset' contains 2GB of double precision numbers chunk_1 = h5read('myFile.h5','/dset',[1 1],[numDouble 1]); chunk_2 = h5read('myFile.h5','/dset',[numDouble+1 1],[numDouble 1]); % Concatenate the two chunks wholeDataset = [chunk_1; chunk_2]; % Clear temporary variables clear chunk_1 chunk_2

Summary: NDWT and NDWT2 return incorrect results
Description: NDWT  and NDWT2  do not decompose the signal or image as expected with the wavelet transform. In the wavelet transform, increasing the level provides a coarser scale (lower frequency) analysis of a signal or image. NDWT  and NDWT2  do not provide this result. Increasing the level in NDWT  and NDWT2  has no effect on the output.
Workaround: For 1-D data, use SWT if 2^J divides the length of your data. J is the level of the wavelet transform. For 2-D data, use SWT2 if 2^J divides both the row and column dimension of your image. J is the level of the wavelet transform.

Summary: Logging output signals of blocks whose names include non-ASCII characters names can cause problems in Rapid Accelerator mode
Description: In Rapid Accelerator mode, logging a signal connected to the outports of a block whose name includes non-ASCII characters names may cause problems. On Windows computers, the following error might be thrown while building the model:  Unable to build a standalone executable to simulate the model 'mTestModel' in rapid accelerator mode. On Linux or Mac computers, the signal may be logged with an incorrect block path, which can result in having multiple signals logged in a single Dataset  object that have the same block path and port index. This can make it difficult to identify the exact source of the logged data on the model.
Workaround: Consider replacing non-ASCII block names with unique ASCII names.

Summary: Linux: MATLAB? CPU usage spikes when MATLAB is idle
Description: Using the Linux top  command, you can see that MATLAB is using a lot of the CPU, even when MATLAB is not running any programs. After running MATLAB for about an hour, the MATLAB CPU usage spikes for 2-5 seconds, and then drops for a few seconds. This cycle repeats until MATLAB is closed.
Workaround: At the MATLAB command prompt, run:com.mathworks.mlwidgets.html.HtmlComponentFactory.setDefaultType('HTMLRENDERER'); Undo the previous command with this command: com.mathworks.mlwidgets.html.HtmlComponentFactory.setDefaultType([]);

Summary: Code generation might fail for MATLAB Function blocks in Model Reference blocks
Description: When you use a MATLAB Function block in a Model Reference block, code generation might fail with the following error.  "Error calling generated SFunction. Stateflow Internal Error: rtw_optimization info did not return a valid chart_inlinable property."

Summary: contourf plot contains repeated or unused colors
Description: In a filled contour plot created with the contourf  function, some colors from the colormap might be repeated while others are left unused. This typically happens when the number of colors in the colormap is less than or equal to the number of contour intervals.

Summary: Error occurs when opening a MATLAB-related file within Windows Explorer
Description: If MATLAB is not running and you use Windows Explorer to open a MATLAB-related file (for example, fm_nonemlfix_pil.mdl ), an error message displays. The message reports that the file can not be found or does not exist, even though the file does exist. This action starts MATLAB, but the file does not open within MATLAB. Once MATLAB is running, you can open the file within Explorer, although the error message might be displayed again. If you quit MATLAB and repeat these steps, the problem might not occur, but this result could be temporary.
Workaround: Defragment the operating system disk (drive C:) by running the Disk Defragmenter on Windows 7 or Diskeeper on XP.

Summary: The Position property of the axes title is not saved when a figure is saved and then loaded.
Description: The issue occurs when the Position  property of the axes title is changed with title  function and the figure is saved. When the figure is loaded, the title's Position  property is reset to default. The following code demonstrates the issue: figure;title('hello', 'Position', [1 1]);t = findall(1, 'Type', 'text');saveas(gcf, 'hello'); get(t, 'Position')f = openfig('hello');t = findall(f, 'Type', 'text');get(t, 'Position') The Position  property of the title is reset to the default value.
Workaround: Change the title's Position property after loading the figure:f = openfig('hello');set(get(gca,'title'),'Position',[1 1]);

Summary: Text spanning multiple lines in a textbox annotation may extend below the bounding box of the annotation when the VerticalAlignment property is set to bottom or baseline.
Description: The following example illustrates the problem.  fig=figure; a=annotation(fig,'textbox',... 'String',{'This is a'... 'multiline string'}); set(a,'VerticalAlignment','bottom')

Summary: MATLAB cancels Log Out, Shut Down, or Restart on Macintosh OS X.
Description: If you try to Log Out, Shut Down, or Restart, the command is canceled, and the following message appears:  The application MATLAB canceled shut down.
Workaround: Quit MATLAB before using Log Out, Shut Down, or Restart.

Summary: CLIM value for CONTOUR function has regressed
Description: Execute the following in MATLAB 6.5 (R13) and MATLAB 7.x (in or after R14): cxz = [0.95 0.89 0.82 0.92; ...       0.23 0.76 0.44 0.74; ...       0.61 0.46 0.62 0.18; ...       0.49 0.02 0.79 0.41];imagesc(cxz);hold on;contour(cxz+32,'w');get(gca,'clim') In MATLAB 6.5, the result is [0.02 0.95], and in MATLAB 7.x, the result is [0.02 32.90].
Workaround: The Workaround in MATLAB 7.x is to use the CAXIS command:cxz = [0.95 0.89 0.82 0.92; ...       0.23 0.76 0.44 0.74; ...       0.61 0.46 0.62 0.18; ...       0.49 0.02 0.79 0.41];imagesc(cxz);hold on;cx = caxis;contour(cxz+32,'w');caxis(cx);get(gca,'clim')In MATLAB 7.x, the result is [0.02 0.95].
